source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Asthma – 38
source.population: People presenting with suspected or established asthma in the populations specified by the guideline.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for Dubai telehealth asthma documentation; emergency management and controlled-medicine prescribing are excluded and in-person use remains partial.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Defines DHA telehealth scope, licensed-facility applicability, and emergency and controlled-medication exclusions.

assessment.text: Supports inhaler-technique and adherence review at every review and clinician-documented personalised action-plan and trigger discussion without mapping medicine names or doses.

rewrites.final_wording: Supports sudden or severe breathing difficulty, cardiac or chest symptoms, cyanosis, inability to speak full sentences, confusion or agitation, stridor or audible wheeze, immobility or trauma, orthopnoea, status asthmaticus, and near-fatal asthma history as clinician-assessed prompts.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Asthma – 38
source.population: People presenting with suspected or established asthma in the populations specified by the guideline.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for Dubai telehealth asthma documentation; emergency management and controlled-medicine prescribing are excluded and in-person use remains partial.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Defines DHA telehealth scope, licensed-facility applicability, and emergency and controlled-medication exclusions.

rewrites.final_wording: Supports review of alternative or comorbid causes, adherence, inhaler technique, smoking, occupational, psychosocial and environmental factors, treatment response, device review, and clinician-led referral.
